summary: A solid soundboard capture of Jerry’s first night back from laryngitis, highlighted by a bouncy first set and a sizzling “Other One” in the second.
review: The recording is a fairly clean sound‑board source with good instrument separation, though occasional microphone feedback squawks and a brief cut on “Around & A” remind you it isn’t flawless. Musically the band delivers an energetic show – the first set rolls strong with “Friend of the Devil,” “Cassidy,” “Music” and a rocking “Passenger,” while the second set shines on a hot “The Other One” and a lively “Franklin’s Tower.” Overall the performance is upbeat despite Jerry’s still‑recovering vocals.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 4‑star ratings, praising both the musical flow and the soundboard quality; minor recording glitches keep it just shy of a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear instrument separation and strong mix; occasional mic‑feedback squawks, a brief drop‑out on “Around & A,” and overall sound not as polished as the very best SBD releases.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds a buoyant momentum that peaks with “Music,” while the second set starts a bit hesitant but gains fire through “The Other One” and closes strong on “Franklin’s Tower.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Back from laryngitis – vocals tentative but energetic; guitar work remains powerful.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass ‘bombs’ are highlighted as a key strength of the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Adds his signature China Cat lick during the “Franklin’s” jam and contributes solid rhythm playing.
